description: STATEHOOD TALK Senator Quay Claims to Have Something Up His Sleeve NO LEGISLATION Tbii Beitlon For Either Indian Territory Or Oklahoma Omnibus Bill Will Be Talked to Death Webster Ballanger the Wash logtoo correspondent of a comber of Indian Territory newspapers writes nnder date of January 8 as follows There is absolutely no hope for legislation in the interest of Indian Territory at this session of congress says Representative Bherman of New York chairman of the committee on Indian affairs of the boose of representatives lean assure you that we have done all in our power to acoom plinth more than the regular appro prlatioua but other bueinesss nasi brushed us aside Among the best friends of In dian Territory continued Mr bherman re Congressman Cur lis of Kansas and Congressman Little of Arkansas Those gentle men seem to have clearer under landing of the needs and rights of Indian I errltory than any others who come here to legislate for the territory but there are many other ardent friends of Indian Territory who have dona their best only to be brushed aside You see when ooogresa is obliged to look after national appropriation bills to prepare to tackle the
